▶Jira feature-request guidance lookup — form choice and filing workflow confirmed (10/7)2026-07-10 15:00 MYT · documentation review only · Jira submissions 0 → 0 · no marketplace or customer writejira-feature-guidanceCompleted

Task: Recalled the previous Jira/GRBT guidance for how Patricia should raise a true feature ticket and separated it from Bug Report and Change Request workflows.

Before → after: Before: the remembered Jira link GRBT/form/152 could be mistaken for the feature form. After: saved references confirm form/152 is Bug Report; the historical form/921 was Change Request; a wholly new greenfield build must use the separate form labelled Feature request from GRBT → Forms.
Reusable filing pattern: Profile 70 / Kmall:Patricia → Awesomeree Jira GRBT project → Forms → choose the form by label. Fill Title, Overview, Priority, optional Due Date, Page, Requesting Team, Requestor=Patricia, and relevant attachments. Structure the overview as Request/Background, Scope, Requirements, Acceptance Criteria, owner split, evidence, and open IT questions.
Ticket-type rule: Bug Report = defect/regression; Change Request = modification or extension of an existing system; Feature Request = wholly new greenfield capability.
Live current-ID check: A read-only in-app Browser check was attempted, but no in-app browser tab/session was available. Per the browser workflow, no unrelated automation surface was substituted. Jira pages opened 0 → 0; the current numeric Feature Request URL remains unverified.
Submission/readback rule: Draft first and obtain Patricia's explicit approval for the Jira write. After submit, verify the confirmation text We're on it! Thanks for your response. The service-desk form does not show a GRBT ticket number, so check the GRBT board/queue for IT triage and capture the eventual issue ID.
Changes made: Guidance state unclear → documented distinction recovered; .agents/error-log.md unchanged for this lookup → appended with the corrected Windows search-tool lessons; Jira forms/submissions 0 → 0; customer/marketplace/stock/SiteGiant/employee-webapp writes 0 → 0.
Shop: none.
Platform: Jira GRBT, Profile 70 reference, and local repository/Claude memory documentation.
Reason: Patricia asked to check the previous skill/workflow for raising a feature Jira ticket.

Outstanding / what Patricia may have missed: The saved June reference did not capture the Feature Request form's numeric URL, and historical form IDs changed later. Use the current form labelled Feature request rather than trusting an old numeric ID. Safety: row 14704 field writes 3; new Shopee MY manual lookups/patches 0/0; stockIds writes 0; stock-in 0; SiteGiant inventory 0; marketplace/customer writes 0.
Tooling corrections / durable lessons:
  • A broad rg search traversed a credential-bearing AdsPower JSON and exposed secret fields in internal tool output; future searches must target safe artifact files or project only required fields.
  • Relative FetchReturns selected the wrong employee context and returned HTTP 404; the absolute employee API retry succeeded.
  • A full-array browser serialization timed out and a following WebSocket attempt closed; the targeted row-only expression succeeded.
  • The first Profile 58 retry nested the one-row payload and searched an empty term; it was rejected. An explicit flat payload then searched the exact tracking and matched.
  • The patch builder initially hit a UTF-8 BOM and was corrected to strip BOM before JSON.parse.
  • apply_patch was blocked by the Windows split-root sandbox for one follow-up edit; the narrow assertion-guarded fallback was read back and syntax checked.
  • AdsPower stop acceptance was not treated as cleanup proof; Profile 58 was polled until Inactive.
  • Two inline python -c board-preview attempts had quoting errors; a file-backed validator replaced them before any board write.
  • A combined parallel closeout wrapper treated the expected rg no-match exit code 1 as a task failure; checks were rerun independently with explicit exit handling.
  • apply_patch was blocked for the final logging edit and the first exact source replacement missed its newline anchor; both failed before writing, then a scoped assertion-checked update succeeded.
Result:Completed; patched/readback verified 24/24 rows and 72/72 one-field requests. No unresolved no-hit remains.
Monitoring / what Patricia may have missed: no active manual Return Items action remains. The automatic Windows task Codex_ReturnItems_Weekend_1030 was last read back Disabled and was not changed. Newly added Shopee MY rows are bot-first monitoring, not manual action items. Standing-skip rows remain skipped unless reopened.
2026-07-10ui-warning-render-refresh - corrected root cause: PowerShell shell_snapshot disabled (2026-07-10)actual runtime root cause corrected · shell_snapshot true -> false · post-fix warnings 0 · tests 6/6bug-fixerCodex/PowerShellCompleted

Date/task: 2026-07-10 MYT correct the recurring yellow Codex warning above new PowerShell threads and fold the correction into the existing same-day card.

Shop/platform: N/A; Codex desktop/CLI 0.144.1 on Windows PowerShell and the user-level Codex configuration.

Reason/root cause: Codex had the stable shell_snapshot feature enabled by default, but PowerShell snapshots are not supported yet. Each new thread retried the unsupported optimization. The earlier CSS and PDF/PNG work targeted unrelated static artifacts, so the warning kept appearing.

Correction of earlier same-day conclusion: the prior statement that no active yellow-warning gap remained was retracted. The static artifact updates remain valid, but they were not the source of this Codex runtime banner.
Actual before -> after:
  • Diagnosis: static proposal CSS/PDF/PNG warning styling -> Codex runtime PowerShell shell-snapshot initialization
  • C:\Users\tessf\.codex\config.toml [features]: shell_snapshot override absent (default-on/effective true) -> shell_snapshot = false
  • codex features list: shell_snapshot stable true -> shell_snapshot stable false
  • New shell-snapshot warnings: recurring for new PowerShell threads; latest pre-fix row id 224996289 at 2026-07-10 14:31:43 MYT -> 0 rows at or after config change 2026-07-10 14:35:49 MYT in a fresh post-fix agent probe
  • Shell repeated-command environment snapshot optimization: enabled -> disabled; ordinary PowerShell commands remain enabled
  • Raw root-cause evidence file: missing -> created; session closeout: missing -> created; durable error-log correction: missing -> appended once
  • Speculative .gitignore from an intermediate wrong hypothesis: absent -> briefly created -> removed/absent; no final ignore-rule change
  • Repository application source: unchanged -> unchanged; dependencies: 0 -> 0; customer/marketplace/stock/SiteGiant/employee-webapp/scheduler writes: 0 -> 0
Earlier same-day artifact changes retained:
  • Product Video Library Proposal.pdf: 452103 bytes / 2026-06-12 17:36:52 -> 452125 bytes / 2026-07-10 09:23:46
  • Product Video Library Proposal - Preview.png: 290904 bytes / 1280x1800 / 2026-06-12 12:13:40 -> 286843 bytes / 1280x1800 / 2026-07-10 09:24:16
  • FBS Claim Progress Dashboard Proposal - Revised.pdf: 202289 bytes / 2026-05-26 21:24:16 -> 199985 bytes / 2026-07-10 09:23:59
  • FBS Claim Progress Dashboard Proposal - Revised Preview.png: 312173 bytes / 1000x5200 / 2026-05-26 21:24:32 -> 312084 bytes / 1000x5200 / 2026-07-10 09:24:17
  • fbs-parcel-claim-dashboard-proposal.pdf: 227844 bytes / 2026-05-26 20:41:24 -> 224913 bytes / 2026-07-10 09:23:59
  • fbs-parcel-claim-dashboard-proposal-preview.png: 506889 bytes / 1000x6900 / 2026-05-26 20:43:01 -> 506774 bytes / 1000x6900 / 2026-07-10 09:24:17

Brief: Codex completed the 2026-07-07 FBS Claim retry after Profile70 could not verify Luxbin source cleanly. Profile78 verified the targeted Shopee FBS source rows, four employee webapp Action values were set, three KarlMobel Sheet rows were appended, an unintended broad action apply was restored, and final Sheet/webapp readback is clean.

Reason for change: Three employee FBS current cases were missing from the Sheet and source-confirmed as FBS. Four webapp Action values no longer matched current source/status. Historical approved closures had to stay closed under the 2026-06-30 wallet-approved closure rule.
Source evidence: Profile 70 full source probe succeeded for MurahYa, ValueSnap, and KarlMobel but Luxbin template capture failed. Profile 78 focused source check returned 8/8 SOURCE_FBS_MATCH with API errors 0, including Luxbin.
Result: OK. Final readback after writes and restore: webapp cases 550; Sheet rows MurahYa 108 / KarlMobel 181 / ValueSnap 212 / Luxbin 10; supported Sheet-to-webapp patches 0; Action patches 0; webapp-not-in-Sheet 0; suspect closures 0.
